question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker who is NOT authorized by `taker` call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral(targetUnits, maxBuyerAssets, taker=victim, ..., collateralWithdrawals=[{collateralIndex, assets}], collateralReceiver=attacker) under the precondition that victim has authorized MidnightBundles on Midnight but has NOT authorized attacker, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler checks isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) -> check passes only if attacker is authorized -> assert the require(taker == msg.sender || isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender)) gate cannot be bypassed by any msg.sender who is not taker and not authorized, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal requires explicit victim authorization, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure from victim's position to attacker-controlled collateralReceiver? Proof idea: unit test with attacker != taker and isAuthorized(taker, attacker) == false, assert revert with Unauthorized().

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an authorized operator (msg.sender authorized by taker on Midnight) call buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with collateralReceiver=attacker_wallet under the precondition that taker has authorized the bundler AND the operator on Midnight, triggering the sequence: operator calls bundler -> bundler passes isAuthorized check -> bundler calls withdrawCollateral(market, index, assets, taker, collateralReceiver=attacker_wallet) -> Midnight checks isAuthorized(taker, bundler) -> collateral sent to attacker_wallet, violating the invariant that an authorized operator should not be able to redirect victim collateral to an arbitrary third-party address, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure to attacker-controlled receiver? Proof idea: fuzz collateralReceiver across all addresses and assert collateral only reaches taker or taker-approved addresses.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an authorized operator call repayAndWithdrawCollateral(market, assets, onBehalf=victim, loanTokenPermit, collateralWithdrawals=[{index, assets}], collateralReceiver=attacker, referralFeePct, referralFeeRecipient) under the precondition that victim has authorized the bundler and the operator on Midnight, triggering the sequence: operator calls bundler -> bundler checks isAuthorized(onBehalf, msg.sender) -> bundler calls repay(market, units, onBehalf, address(0), '') -> bundler calls withdrawCollateral(market, index, assets, onBehalf, collateralReceiver=attacker) -> Midnight checks isAuthorized(onBehalf, bundler) -> collateral sent to attacker, violating the invariant that repay+withdraw must only benefit the position owner or their explicitly chosen receiver, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure after forced debt reduction? Proof idea: PoC where operator repays victim's debt and redirects all collateral to attacker address.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker call supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget(targetUnits, minSellerAssets, taker=victim, receiver=attacker, collateralSupplies=[], takes=[...], referralFeePct=0, referralFeeRecipient=attacker) under the precondition that victim has authorized the bundler and the attacker on Midnight, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler checks isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) -> bundler calls take(..., taker, receiverIfTakerIsSeller=address(this), ...) -> loan assets flow to bundler -> bundler transfers filledSellerAssets to receiver=attacker, violating the invariant that loan assets from selling (borrowing) must only reach the taker or taker-designated receiver, causing scoped impact: unauthorized borrowing with loan proceeds redirected to attacker? Proof idea: unit test where attacker is authorized by victim, assert loan assets reach attacker not victim.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an attacker call supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget(targetSellerAssets, maxUnits, taker=victim, receiver=attacker, collateralSupplies=[{index, assets, permit}], takes=[...], referralFeePct, referralFeeRecipient) under the precondition that victim has authorized the bundler and the attacker on Midnight and the attacker has approved the bundler to pull collateral from msg.sender (attacker), triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler pulls collateral from attacker -> bundler supplies collateral to victim's position -> bundler takes sell offers on behalf of victim -> loan assets sent to receiver=attacker, violating the invariant that debt increase on victim's position must only occur with victim's explicit consent for the specific receiver, causing scoped impact: victim's position gains debt while attacker receives loan assets? Proof idea: fuzz receiver address and assert victim's debt increases while attacker receives assets.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the coarse-grained Midnight authorization model by first getting victim to authorize the bundler, then calling buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with takes[0].offer.market.enterGate=address(0) while the victim's intended market has enterGate set, under the precondition that the bundler is authorized by victim and the attacker crafts a market struct with enterGate=address(0) that hashes to a different id, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with crafted market -> touchMarket creates new market with no gate -> take executes in ungated market -> collateral withdrawn from victim's position in original market, violating the invariant that gate checks must apply to all entry paths including bundler-mediated ones, causing scoped impact: gate bypass allowing unauthorized credit/debt increase? Proof idea: differential test comparing direct take vs bundler-mediated take with mismatched market gate.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker manipulate the referralFeePct and referralFeeRecipient parameters to cause an arithmetic underflow in the final transfer `SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets)` under the precondition that referralFeePct is close to WAD-1 and filledBuyerAssets is large, triggering the sequence: attacker sets referralFeePct = WAD-1 -> referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets * (WAD-1) / 1 which overflows or exceeds maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets -> subtraction underflows in unchecked context or reverts, violating the invariant that the bundler must always return exactly maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets to msg.sender without underflow, causing scoped impact: fund freeze or incorrect token accounting in the bundler? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct in [0, WAD-1] and filledBuyerAssets in [0, maxBuyerAssets] and assert no underflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker who is authorized by taker call the bundler with takes[] where takes[0].offer.market differs from takes[i].offer.market for i>0 (but all hash to the same id via a crafted collision or the InconsistentMarket check is insufficient) under the precondition that the market id check uses toId() which depends on the full market struct, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> id = touchMarket(takes[0].offer.market) -> for i>0, require(toId(takes[i].offer.market) == id) -> collateralWithdrawals use takes[0].offer.market -> if takes[0] market differs from the market where taker has collateral, withdrawCollateral may operate on wrong market, violating the invariant that all operations in a bundle must target the same market, causing scoped impact: collateral withdrawn from unintended market or wrong collateral params applied? Proof idea: unit test with takes[0] market having different collateralParams than takes[1] market but same id.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker pass an empty takes[] array to trigger an index-out-of-bounds revert at takes[0].offer.market.loanToken before the authorization check is reached, under the precondition that the authorization check at line 60 comes AFTER takes[0] access at line 62, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with takes=[] -> line 62 accesses takes[0] -> revert with array index out of bounds -> authorization check never reached, violating the invariant that authorization must be checked before any state-reading operations that could leak information, causing scoped impact: DoS or information leak about array access ordering? Proof idea: unit test with empty takes array and assert revert reason is array OOB not Unauthorized.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker who is authorized by onBehalf call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with assets=0 and collateralWithdrawals=[{index, assets=largeAmount}] under the precondition that onBehalf has zero debt (so repay of 0 units succeeds as a no-op) and has collateral deposited, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, 0, permit) -> forceApproveMax -> repay(market, 0, onBehalf, address(0), '') -> withdrawCollateral(market, index, largeAmount, onBehalf, collateralReceiver=attacker) -> collateral sent to attacker, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must require valid debt repayment or explicit owner action, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure via zero-repay bundle? Proof idea: PoC with assets=0 and non-zero collateralWithdrawals, assert collateral reaches attacker.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker supply collateral to a victim's position using collateralSupplies with a malicious collateral token that reenters the bundler during transferFrom, under the precondition that the collateral token is an ERC777-like token with a hook on transferFrom, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler calls pullToken(maliciousToken, attacker, assets, permit) -> maliciousToken.transferFrom triggers hook -> hook reenters bundler with different taker -> bundler's forceApproveMax has already approved MIDNIGHT -> reentrant call executes take on behalf of different taker, violating the invariant that reentrancy must not corrupt partial state or allow unauthorized takes, causing scoped impact: unauthorized borrowing or collateral seizure via ERC777 reentrancy? Proof idea: mock ERC777 collateral token that reenters bundler in transferFrom hook.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker exploit the try/catch pattern around take() to cause the bundler to withdraw collateral after zero successful takes by crafting all takes to revert, then passing targetUnits=0, under the precondition that targetUnits=0 satisfies the loop exit condition filledUnits < targetUnits immediately (0 < 0 is false), triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with targetUnits=0 and non-empty collateralWithdrawals -> loop exits immediately with filledUnits=0 -> require(filledUnits == targetUnits) passes (0==0) -> collateralWithdrawals loop executes -> withdrawCollateral called for each entry, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must be contingent on successful take execution, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal without any corresponding borrow/lend? Proof idea: unit test with targetUnits=0 and non-empty collateralWithdrawals, assert withdrawCollateral is called.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker exploit targetUnits=0 to supply collateral to victim's position without executing any takes, under the precondition that attacker is authorized by victim and has approved the bundler to pull collateral, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with targetUnits=0 and non-empty collateralSupplies -> collateral supplied to victim's position -> loop exits immediately -> require(filledUnits == targetUnits) passes (0==0) -> no takes executed -> referralFee=0 -> filledSellerAssets=0 transferred to receiver, violating the invariant that collateral supply to a victim's position must only occur as part of a valid borrow operation, causing scoped impact: attacker can activate collateral slots on victim's position (up to MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER limit) causing TooManyActivatedCollaterals DoS? Proof idea: unit test with targetUnits=0 and 16 collateralSupplies, assert victim's collateral bitmap is poisoned.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker manipulate the referralFeeAssets computation to cause targetFilledBuyerAssets to underflow when referralFeePct is close to WAD, under the precondition that referralFeePct = WAD-1 and targetBuyerAssets = 1, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with referralFeePct=WAD-1 and targetBuyerAssets=1 -> referralFeeAssets = 1 * (WAD-1) / WAD = 0 (rounded down) -> targetFilledBuyerAssets = 1 - 0 = 1 -> loop runs with targetFilledBuyerAssets=1 -> referralFeeAssets=0 so no referral fee sent -> msg.sender gets no refund, violating the invariant that referral fee computation must be consistent between the pre-loop calculation and the actual assets transferred, causing scoped impact: referral fee accounting desync leading to incorrect token distribution? Proof idea: fuzz targetBuyerAssets and referralFeePct and assert referralFeeAssets + refund + filledBuyerAssets == targetBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the referralFeeAssets computation asymmetry where referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets * referralFeePct / (WAD - referralFeePct) to cause targetFilledSellerAssets to overflow when referralFeePct approaches WAD, under the precondition that referralFeePct = WAD-1 and targetSellerAssets is large, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler with referralFeePct=WAD-1 -> referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets * (WAD-1) / 1 -> overflow in mulDivDown -> revert or wrap, violating the invariant that referral fee computation must not overflow for any valid referralFeePct < WAD, causing scoped impact: DoS of the sell bundle function or incorrect fee accounting? Proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets and referralFeePct and assert no overflow in referralFeeAssets computation.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: pullToken (ERC2612 path)] Can an attacker front-run a victim's bundler call to consume the ERC2612 permit before the bundler uses it, causing the bundler to fall back to safeTransferFrom which requires a pre-existing allowance, under the precondition that victim signed an ERC2612 permit for the bundler and submitted a bundler transaction, triggering the sequence: attacker sees victim's pending tx -> attacker calls token.permit(victim, bundler, amount, deadline, v, r, s) -> victim's tx executes -> bundler tries permit (fails silently) -> bundler calls safeTransferFrom(token, victim, bundler, amount) -> if victim has no pre-existing allowance, safeTransferFrom reverts, violating the invariant that ERC2612 permit-based bundler calls must be atomic and not front-runnable, causing scoped impact: DoS of victim's bundler transaction? Proof idea: unit test simulating front-run permit consumption, assert bundler reverts on safeTransferFrom.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: pullToken (ERC2612 path)] Can an attacker exploit the silent permit failure in pullToken to cause the bundler to pull tokens from msg.sender without consuming the permit, then replay the permit in a subsequent call, under the precondition that the permit was not consumed (e.g., because the bundler already had sufficient allowance), triggering the sequence: first bundler call -> permit try fails silently -> safeTransferFrom uses existing allowance -> permit remains valid -> second bundler call -> permit try succeeds -> safeTransferFrom pulls additional tokens, violating the invariant that each ERC2612 permit must be consumed exactly once, causing scoped impact: double-pull of tokens from victim? Proof idea: unit test with pre-existing allowance and valid permit, assert permit is consumed on first call.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: forceApproveMax] Can an attacker exploit the bundler's persistent max approval to MIDNIGHT to drain tokens left in the bundler from a previous call, under the precondition that a previous bundler call left residual loan tokens in the bundler (e.g., due to a failed transfer or rounding), triggering the sequence: previous call leaves N tokens in bundler -> attacker calls any bundler function with maxBuyerAssets=0 -> bundler calls forceApproveMax(loanToken, MIDNIGHT) -> MIDNIGHT already has max approval -> attacker uses a crafted take to pull bundler's residual balance via MIDNIGHT's transferFrom, violating the invariant that the bundler must not hold residual token balances between calls, causing scoped impact: theft of residual tokens from bundler? Proof idea: unit test that leaves tokens in bundler and assert subsequent call cannot extract them.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker who is authorized by taker use the bundler to call Midnight.setIsAuthorized on behalf of taker (since the bundler is authorized by taker and Midnight's authorization is coarse-grained), under the precondition that taker has authorized the bundler on Midnight and the bundler's authorization allows calling any Midnight function, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler is authorized by taker -> attacker crafts a take with a malicious maker callback that calls Midnight.setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, taker) via the bundler's authorization -> attacker gains full authorization over taker's position, violating the invariant that bundler authorization must not transitively grant arbitrary Midnight permissions, causing scoped impact: unauthorized full control over victim's Midnight position? Proof idea: PoC where maker callback calls setIsAuthorized through bundler's authorization.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker craft takes with offers where offer.ratifier is a malicious contract that reenters the bundler during isRatified(), under the precondition that the bundler has already pulled maxBuyerAssets from msg.sender and approved MIDNIGHT, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler pulls tokens -> bundler calls take(offer, ratifierData, ...) -> Midnight calls ratifier.isRatified(offer, ratifierData) -> malicious ratifier reenters bundler with different parameters -> reentrant call executes withdrawCollateral or another take -> outer call continues with stale state, violating the invariant that ratifier callbacks must not corrupt bundler state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral withdrawal or double-take via ratifier reentrancy? Proof idea: mock malicious ratifier that reenters bundler, assert state consistency after reentrant call.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the fact that collateralSupplies are processed before takes to supply collateral to victim's position and then have all takes fail (caught by try/catch), leaving victim with extra collateral but no debt reduction, under the precondition that attacker is authorized by victim and crafts takes that all revert, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> collateral supplied to victim's position -> all takes fail silently -> require(filledUnits == targetUnits) reverts with OutOfOffers -> entire transaction reverts -> collateral supply is also reverted, violating the invariant that failed bundles must fully revert without partial state changes, causing scoped impact: no impact if revert is clean, but verify atomicity? Proof idea: unit test with all-failing takes and assert full revert including collateral supply.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker pass takes where takes[i].offer.market has a different enterGate than takes[0].offer.market, causing the bundler to call touchMarket on takes[0].offer.market (creating it with no gate) while the InconsistentMarket check uses toId() which includes the gate in the hash, under the precondition that the attacker crafts two market structs with different enterGate values but the same other fields, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> id = touchMarket(takes[0].offer.market) with enterGate=address(0) -> for i>0, toId(takes[i].offer.market) != id because enterGate differs -> InconsistentMarket revert, violating the invariant that market id must be consistent across all takes, causing scoped impact: DoS of bundler or gate bypass if id collision is possible? Proof idea: differential test comparing toId() with different enterGate values.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker who is authorized by onBehalf call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with a fee-on-transfer loan token, causing the bundler to pull `assets` from msg.sender but only receive `assets - fee` due to transfer fee, then repay `units = assets - referralFeeAssets` which is more than the bundler holds, under the precondition that the loan token charges a transfer fee on transferFrom, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> pullToken pulls assets but bundler receives assets-fee -> forceApproveMax -> repay(market, units=assets-referralFeeAssets, ...) -> Midnight pulls units from bundler via transferFrom -> bundler has insufficient balance -> revert, violating the invariant that fee-on-transfer tokens must not cause accounting desync between pulled and repaid amounts, causing scoped impact: DoS of repay bundle with fee-on-transfer tokens? Proof idea: mock fee-on-transfer token and assert bundler handles balance correctly.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an attacker exploit the fact that the bundler sends exactly `targetSellerAssets` to receiver (not `filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets`) to extract value when filledSellerAssets > targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets due to rounding in sellerAssetsToUnits, under the precondition that TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits returns units that yield more sellerAssets than targetFilledSellerAssets due to mulDivDown rounding, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> sellerAssetsToUnits computes units -> take yields filledSellerAssets > targetFilledSellerAssets -> require(filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets) reverts with OutOfOffers -> or if equal, bundler holds excess -> excess stays in bundler, violating the invariant that all loan assets received by bundler must be fully distributed to receiver and referralFeeRecipient, causing scoped impact: residual loan tokens locked in bundler? Proof idea: fuzz tick/units/price and assert filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets exactly.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Critical Unauthorized borrowing, withdrawal, collateral seizure, or debt reduction caused by broken authorization, signature, ratifier, gate, or callback logic'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an attacker pass takes with offers where offer.callback is a malicious contract that reenters the bundler during the take's buyer callback, under the precondition that the bundler has already pulled maxBuyerAssets and approved MIDNIGHT, triggering the sequence: attacker calls bundler -> bundler calls take(offer, ...) -> Midnight executes buyer callback (offer.callback) -> malicious callback reenters bundler.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with different parameters -> inner call executes withdrawCollateral -> outer call continues and also executes withdrawCollateral -> double withdrawal, violating the invariant that callback reentrancy must not allow double collateral withdrawal, causing scoped impact: unauthorized double collateral seizure via maker callback reentrancy? Proof idea: mock malicious maker callback that reenters bundler, assert collateral withdrawn at most once.
